learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate the skills and competencies required to work competently and confidently with low to moderate risk clients; and recognise the need for referral to other related professionals when appropriate. As described within the scope of practice by Exercise and Sport Science Australia (ESSA) for Exercise Science (400 hours minimum)
Demonstrate professional conduct that is sensitive to client diversity and equity and is consistent with the ESSA Code of Professional Conduct and Ethical Practice
Develop scientific rationale for professional decision making and service delivery, appropriate time management and ability to work collaboratively, and independently (as appropriate)

Demonstrate the ability to use research skills to critically evaluate specific areas of assessment, client centered interventions and/or education to determine whether research evidence indicates that current practice should be maintained or modified
Demonstrate effective written and oral communication skills relevant to the profession

assessments.due_date: 5:00 pm on the Friday of Week 3 of placement.
assessments.task_description:
To meet HMST4314 requirements during your placement, a logbook must be kept detailing all relevant practicum hours. A minimum of 400 hours of practicum is required across a range of sports science activities to demonstrate competency in assessment, analysis, programming, and delivery within the Accredited Sports Scientist (ASpS) scope of practice. Practicum hours must be completed across multiple sports science disciplines, with at least 120 hours involving direct service delivery to athletes or sport science service users. The remaining hours may include assessment or analysis activities that reflect the ASpS scope of practice. 

The initial logbook submission is used to check that your practicum hours are being recorded in line with logbook reporting requirements.
assessments.submission_guidelines: For this assessment, you must complete your first three weeks of placement logbook entries using the provided logbook template. Once completed, upload your logbook template to ePortfolio using the submission link available on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: 5:00 pm, 14 calendar days following placement completion date
assessments.task_description: The final logbook submission requires you to submit your completed logbook, with all practicum hours recorded in accordance with the relevant logbook reporting requirements.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Once completed, upload your logbook template to ePortfolio using the submission link available on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: 5:00 pm on the Friday of your third week of placement.
assessments.task_description: As student practitioners on practicum, you are required to critically reflect on your performance and self-assess your developing capabilities. This self-assessment supports your development as a reflective practitioner, a key capability embedded within the ESSA Professional Practice Standards. You will assess yourself against the ESSA Professional Standards based on your experiences from the first half of your placement.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Click the submission link on Blackboard to access the self-reflection form in ePortfolio. Complete all required sections of the form, including the written reflection responses and self-ratings against the relevant professional attributes.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: 5:00 pm on the Friday of your third week of placement.
assessments.task_description: As student practitioners on practicum, you are required to receive structured feedback on your developing professional capabilities. At the mid-point of your placement, your supervisor will assess your performance against the ESSA Professional Standards and provide ratings and commentary based on your progress to date. The purpose of this assessment is to support your development as a reflective practitioner, guide professional growth, and identify strengths and areas requiring further improvement during the remainder of your placement.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students are responsible for generating the correct assessment link in ePortfolio and sending it to their supervisor. Students must also organise a suitable time to meet with their supervisor to discuss and complete the assessment. Detailed instructions for generating and sharing the link are provided on Blackboard. The supervisor is responsible for completing and submitting the assessment form in ePortfolio.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: 5:00 pm, 14 calendar days following placement completion date
assessments.task_description: At the end of your placement, your supervisor will conduct a Final Professional Attributes Assessment to evaluate your overall performance and readiness for professional practice. This assessment uses the same four-point scale applied throughout your placement. This final assessment provides an opportunity for you and your supervisor to review your performance, acknowledge strengths, identify any remaining areas for development, and confirm that you have met the minimum professional competency required for successful course completion. Unlike the Professional Attributes Intermediate Assessment, the Professional Attributes Final Assessment is summative and contributes to your overall course result. To pass the placement, you must meet or exceed expectations in all Standards.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students are responsible for generating the correct assessment link in ePortfolio and sending it to their supervisor to complete. Students must also organise a suitable time to meet with their supervisor to discuss the assessment. Detailed instructions for generating and sharing the link are provided on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: 5:00 pm, 14 calendar days following placement completion date
assessments.task_description: The Practicum Project provides an opportunity for you to demonstrate applied professional learning during placement by contributing an educational resource relevant to your practicum context. You are required to identify a practice-relevant need within your placement setting and develop one applied educational resource that is relevant to the discipline of Sports Science and appropriate to the context of your placement. You are expected to propose your project idea(s) to your practicum supervisor for discussion and approval. The final educational resource should be informed by your placement experience and developed by you, with consultation from your practicum supervisor, with the aim of supporting clients, athletes, staff, students, or service delivery at the practicum site.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students are responsible for completing their practicum project and uploading the completed assessment to ePortfolio via the submission link available on Blackboard. Detailed instructions for completing and submitting the practicum project are provided on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date: 5:00 pm, 14 calendar days following placement completion date
assessments.task_description: To demonstrate that you have met the ESSA Practicum Standards during your placement, you are required to complete and submit a Record of Student Engagement Form. The Record of Student Engagement provides a structured summary of the activities you undertook during placement, drawing on evidence contained in your practicum logbooks, and a reflective account of how your placement contributed to the development of the ESSA professional attributes required for accreditation. The Record of Student Engagement must be completed using the ESSA-approved template and must be reviewed and signed by your practicum supervisor.
assessments.submission_guidelines: For this assessment, you must complete the ROSE form using the provided template and submit it to your supervisor for review and signature. Once completed, upload your ROSE form to ePortfolio using the submission link available on Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Practicum

Students complete a minimum of 400 placement hours in an appropriate setting to develop an integrated understanding of the of exercise science scope of practice, domains, and sub-discipline.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Practicum Students complete a minimum of 400 placement hours in an appropriate setting to develop an integrated understanding of the of exercise science scope of practice, domains, and sub-discipline.
